expectations.text: Uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params with trailing underscore
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: void Vfosub(const struct sAdamsVforce *vfo, double time, int dflag, int iflag, double *result)

expectations.text: Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
Includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc to query displacement (not a raw function call)
Uses c_errmes for error handling after utility function calls
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_errmes(errflg, "c_sysary TDISP failed in Vfosub", vfo->ID, "STOP");

expectations.text: Parameterizes markers or constants via PAR[] instead of hardcoding marker IDs in the function body
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: K from vfo->PAR[0], free_len from vfo->PAR[1]; markers from vfo->I and vfo->JFLOAT (struct fields, not hardcoded)

expectations.text: Build command uses correct library name adams_util.lib (not adams_utils.lib)
expectations.evidence: Uses mdi.bat cr-u which handles linking automatically; no incorrect library name mentioned.

expectations.text: Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: response.md Build Instructions: 'mdi.bat cr-u n vfosub.c -n my_sub.dll ex' (Windows) and 'mdi -c cr-u n vfosub.c -n my_sub.so ex' (Linux)

expectations.text: Build instructions mention setting up the Adams environment before running mdi.bat
expectations.evidence: response.md shows both automated workflow (python scripts/generate_adams_env.py + call adams_env_init.bat before mdi.bat) and manual workflow (Start Menu → Adams → Command Prompt)
